# API Contracts Checklist: Reviewer-ready PR packet contract

**Purpose**: Validate that PRSG-012's packet schema, rendered Markdown contract, and PR creation command requirements are complete, clear, and consistent before task generation.
**Created**: 2026-06-12
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Required Packet Fields

- [x] CHK001 Are generated title fields defined with final value, type, scope, description, source evidence, and rejected candidates? [Completeness, Spec FR-003, Data Model, Contract]
- [x] CHK002 Is the PR target required in the packet contract so validators can bind generated titles and body files to the exact PR creation target? [Resolved, Spec FR-004A, Data Model PR Packet, Contract]
- [x] CHK003 Is `body_file` constrained as a repo-relative rendered Markdown path rather than any non-empty string? [Resolved, Spec Assumptions, Data Model PR Packet, Contract]
- [x] CHK004 Are required reviewer headings listed in the shared packet contract for Summary, What Changed, Why It Matters, How To Review, How To UAT, Verification, Scope, and Known Gaps? [Completeness, Spec FR-008, Contract]
- [x] CHK005 Does `scope_evidence` include changed-file scope evidence as well as reviewability budget and non-goals? [Resolved, Spec Assumptions, Plan Phase 0 Research Results, Data Model PR Packet, Contract]
- [x] CHK006 Are verification evidence, UAT source, source/provenance markers, editable fields, protected fingerprint, and validation result path represented as required packet fields? [Completeness, Spec FR-010 to FR-016, Contract]

## Single And Split Packet Compatibility

- [x] CHK007 Does split mode require slice identity and source evidence so split PR packets can be traced back to their source slice? [Completeness, Spec Key Entities, Contract]
- [x] CHK008 Does the schema prevent single-PR packets from carrying split-only `split_slice` evidence? [Resolved, Spec Key Entities, Data Model Validation Rules, Contract]
- [x] CHK009 Are single-PR and split-PR title source rules documented without allowing branch names, slice IDs, file paths, or body prose as title sources? [Consistency, Spec FR-003A/FR-003B, Data Model]

## PR Creation Boundary

- [x] CHK010 Is every PR creation path required to use `gh pr create --base <base_branch> --head <head_branch> --title <generated-title> --body-file <generated-body>` before reviewers see the PR? [Completeness, Spec FR-004/FR-005, Plan Summary, Quickstart]
- [x] CHK011 Are validation failures required to block before PR creation and write packet-specific remediation JSON? [Completeness, Spec FR-013/FR-014, Data Model Validation Result]

## Rendered Markdown Contract

- [x] CHK012 Are JSON `required_headings` required to be cross-checked against rendered Markdown headings in the exact canonical order, not only present in packet JSON? [Resolved, Spec FR-008A, Data Model Validation Rules, Contract]
- [x] CHK013 Are source markers required to be rendered provenance outside comments, code fences, generated fixtures, `.process`, and generated zones? [Completeness, Spec FR-010, Assumptions]
- [x] CHK014 Are verification evidence, scope evidence, UAT content, known-gap language, and generated governance evidence protected from non-editable rendered-body changes? [Completeness, Spec FR-011/FR-016A, Data Model Protected Body Fingerprint]
- [x] CHK015 Does the editable-field contract enforce exactly one field for `summary`, `what_changed`, and `why_it_matters`, with matching heading and marker pairs? [Resolved, Data Model Sanctioned Prose Field, Contract]
- [x] CHK016 Are host PR templates explicitly prevented from satisfying or weakening canonical packet sections and protected evidence? [Consistency, Spec FR-016C/FR-017, Research]

## Notes

- Initial API contracts checklist pass produced 6 true gaps for remediation before task generation.
- Re-run pass 1 completed on 2026-06-12 after updating `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `data-model.md`, `quickstart.md`, and `contracts/pr-packet.schema.json`; all six gaps are resolved.
